rationale.R1.1: The abstract makes several stacked contribution claims (first formal analysis, Theorem 3.2, uncertainty estimator pivot). 'Stronger' plausibly targets sharpening these claims and the gap statement — but whether this is the load-bearing sense is unresolved.
rationale.R1.2: The logical chain from bounded-disagreement assumption → Hoeffding → calibration bound → Brier score corollary → empirical confirmation is central to the piece. Strengthening could primarily mean tightening this inferential scaffold.
rationale.R1.3: Three empirical claims (14–22% Brier reduction, 1/sqrt(K) tracking to 6%, rho=0.61 correlation) appear alongside theoretical results. Stronger evidence-claim alignment — flagging where numbers do or do not fully support the theorem — is a plausible core task.
rationale.R1.6: Prose-level strengthening (sharper sentences, punching up the 'most enduring contribution' rhetorical pivot, tightening the abstract's narrative arc) is the other canonical reading of 'make this stronger' for a research abstract.

parsed.clarification_question: By 'stronger,' do you primarily mean (a) more rigorous argumentation — tightening the logical chain from assumptions through Theorem 3.2 to empirical results — or (b) more rhetorically impactful prose — sharpening the contribution framing, sentence quality, and persuasive force of the abstract?
